arts.ttl: Reading team news: Fan favourite returns on the bench for Huddersfield Town trip
arts.sum: Reading favourite Amadou Mbengue has returned to the matchday squad for the first time since January 4 for this afternoon's trip to Huddersfield Town. The 23-year-old last ran out for the Royals against Charlton Athletic in a 0-0 draw at the Valley, damaging his knee. However, after time resting and recuperating, 'Cheeseburger' makes the bench as Noel Hunt names an unchanged starting 11 at the John Smith's Stadium.

arts.ttl: Live updates: Reading look to extend miserable Huddersfield Town winless run
arts.sum: Reading are looking to complete a league double over relegated Huddersfield Town as the Royals return to the John Smith's Stadium for the first time since Championship relegation in 2023. The Terriers are on a three-game losing streak but spent big in the January window eyeing an immediate return to the second tier.

arts.ttl: Saturday's League One predictions including Huddersfield Town vs. Reading
arts.sum: Sports Mole provides score predictions and previews for all of today's League One fixtures, including Huddersfield Town vs. Reading.

arts.ttl: 'Energetic and quality': Huddersfield Town boss on threat posed by visit of Reading
arts.sum: Huddersfield Town boss Michael Duff wants his players to be 'at it' and is wary that Reading could hurt them tomorrow. The Terriers, occupying a play-off space, have lost three League One matches in a row and are only four points ahead of the Royals heading into the clash at the John Smith's Stadium. Back in September, Reading overturned a deficit to defeat Duff's side 2-1 at the SCL Stadium, so a potential league double is on the cards in Yorkshire. Speaking ahead of the visit of Noel Hunt's Royals, Duff spoke of controlling his own side's output, but acknowledged the quality of the Berkshire boys making the journey.

arts.ttl: 'I know what I'm capable of': Young Reading striker on filling 'big' Sam Smith shoes
arts.sum: Reading forward Jayden Wareham is ready to 'show the fans who I am' in the wake of Sam Smith's departure. Smith, 26, made a £2m move to Wrexham on Friday and leaves a void up front, having hit 27 goals in 63 appearances since returning in 2023. The only other recognised number nine is 21-year-old Wareham, who started only his third League One match of the season in his place during Saturday's 1-0 win over Bolton Wanderers. Just like the supporters, Wareham was disappointed to see his teammate depart, but will use it as an opportunity.

arts.ttl: 'We have to be realistic': Reading boss addresses rumour of fan favourite return
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt refused to confirm if former fan favourite Yakou Meite was a target for the winter window, admitting the 'we need to be realistic.' The 28-year-old made nearly 180 appearances for the club between 2016 and 2023, departing following the club's relegation out of the Championship. Working under Hunt, who was interim during that period, Meite scored his final goal in a 1-1 draw with Wigan Athletic, which virtually condemned both clubs to relegation. Unable to find form with new club Cardiff City, the Ivorian has scored once in 25 appearances for the Bluebirds this season. A subject of criticism from the Cardiff supporters, Meite has spoken out about online abuse he has received this season.

arts.ttl: ‘He’s good enough’ – Hull City open to permanent move for loan man as Ruben Selles shares update
arts.sum: Hull City boss Ruben Selles has said he is open to a permanent move for Gustavo Puerta this… The post ‘He’s good enough’ – Hull City open to permanent move for loan man as Ruben Selles shares update appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Reading boss confirms possibility of free agent addition despite EFL transfer embargo
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt has confirmed that there is scope for the club to enter the free agent market to bolster their ranks, despite remaining under a transfer embargo. The Royals have not paid a fee for a player for over five years and will not be able to until they submit their financial accounts, which are almost 12 months overdue. Selling Sam Smith and Tyler Bindon in the window, albeit bringing Bindon back to the club on loan from Nottingham Forest until the summer, Dean Bouzanis also had his contract 'mutually terminated' after becoming surplus to requirements. While loan signings are no longer possible due to the close of the window, Hunt has confirmed that there is the chance of acquiring a free agent, providing they stay within the EFL limitations.

arts.ttl: Reading pair to feature in youth match ahead of long-awaited first-team return
arts.sum: Reading pair Andy Yiadom and Adrian Akande are set to take the next step in their recovery from long-term injury, featuring for the Under-21s tomorrow evening. Club captain Yiadom has been out since April 2024 and is still working his way back from a long-term lay-off while winger Akande has not featured since scoring against Harborough Town in the FA Cup in December. Both have been big misses in what is already a threadbare squad as many other players join them on the absence list. However, both are set to get minutes in the tank on Friday night at Aston Villa while the first-team travel to face Huddersfield Town in League One on Saturday. Ahead of the trip to Yorkshire, manager Noel Hunt spoke of those currently missing and their next plan of action.

arts.ttl: Reading trip to relegation-threatened Crawley amended due to television selection
arts.sum: Reading's trip to Crawley Town has been selected for Sky Sports coverage, pushing kick-off forward. The Royals have never played a league match at the Broadfield Stadium, visiting there last over a decade ago in the FA Cup as a Premier League side in 2013. Expected to sell-out fast for the short trip to Sussex on March 8, it will now kick-off at 12.30pm instead of 3pm. Reading are due to host Birmingham City on Saturday, February 22 and this too is a 12.30pm kick-off live on Sky Sports due to the Blues' push for promotion.
